text.title: GCP Database Patterns
text.summary: Cloud SQL HA configuration, read replica patterns, AlloyDB vs Spanner decision, and backup rules.

rule.statement: Always set availability_type = REGIONAL for production Cloud SQL instances; enable PITR (point-in-time recovery) with 30-day retention; disable public IP.
rule.rationale: ZONAL instances have no automatic failover — a zone outage requires manual intervention. REGIONAL provides automatic failover to a standby in a different zone within 60 seconds.

rule.statement: Enable Query Insights and set logging flags: log_checkpoints, log_connections, log_disconnections, log_lock_waits for PostgreSQL; enable deletion protection on production instances.
rule.rationale: Query Insights identifies slow queries without performance overhead. Connection and lock logging provides the audit trail required for compliance. Deletion protection prevents accidental drops.

rule.statement: Configure SSL enforcement (require_ssl = true); use Private Service Connect for connections from GKE, never expose Cloud SQL via public IP.
rule.rationale: Public IP Cloud SQL instances are frequently found in security audits. Private connectivity eliminates the attack surface for credential stuffing and brute-force attacks.

rule.statement: Create read replicas in the same region as the primary for read-heavy workloads; create cross-region replicas for disaster recovery when RPO is under 1 hour.
rule.rationale: Same-region replicas add no cross-region data transfer costs and reduce primary load. Cross-region replicas provide an independent copy for DR failover.

rule.statement: Use Cloud SQL PostgreSQL for standard OLTP workloads; use AlloyDB for PostgreSQL-compatible workloads requiring 2x+ query throughput; use Spanner for global multi-region consistency with 99.999% SLA.
rule.rationale: AlloyDB delivers 2x transactional and 100x analytical throughput over Cloud SQL via columnar engine. Spanner provides external consistency across regions at significantly higher cost.

antipattern.description: Cloud SQL with availability_type = ZONAL in production.
antipattern.reason: ZONAL provides no automatic failover. A zone outage requires manual promotion of a replica, causing 5-30 minutes of downtime depending on replication lag.
